name: ollama-forge-app
description: Local-first AI coding — a standalone desktop app with Agent and controlled Team workflows on your own hardware via local Ollama.
author: Ollamax

scripts.test: node --test spatial-selection.test.js test/cursor-buddy-state.test.js test/desktop-security.test.js test/hub-input-safety.test.js test/model-output-escaping.test.js test/point-directives.test.js test/voice-runtime.test.js test/workspace-paths.test.js test/package-validation.test.js
scripts.dist: npm run test && npm run validate-runtime && npm run prepare-assets && electron-builder
scripts.dist:mac: npm run test && npm run validate-runtime && npm run prepare-assets && electron-builder --mac
scripts.dist:win: npm run test && npm run validate-runtime && npm run prepare-assets && electron-builder --win
scripts.dist:linux: npm run test && npm run validate-runtime && npm run prepare-assets && electron-builder --linux

//note: node-pty (the terminal backend) is a NATIVE module that needs a per-platform toolchain (it broke CI on macOS distutils + Windows VS detection). It is intentionally NOT a build dependency yet — the app ships without it and the terminal panel degrades gracefully (ide.js shows an install hint). To enable the terminal locally: `npm install node-pty` then `npm run dist`. Re-adding it to the release needs the native build sorted per-OS.

extendInfo.NSMicrophoneUsageDescription: Ollamax uses the microphone only while you hold the local voice-command control. Audio is transcribed on this device.
